vulnerability.name: CVE-2023-45288
vulnerability.description: HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame flood in Go net/http and golang.org/x/net/http2

statements.impact_statement: bindy is written in Rust and does not include the Go runtime or golang.org/x/net/http2. The HTTP/2 server in bindy uses the Rust h2 crate (not Go), which is not affected by this CVE.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-24791
vulnerability.description: net/http: Denial of service due to improper 100-continue handling in Go net/http

statements.impact_statement: bindy is written in Rust and does not use the Go net/http package. HTTP handling is implemented via the Rust axum/hyper stack, which is not affected by this CVE.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2023-0464
vulnerability.description: OpenSSL: Excessive Resource Usage Verifying X.509 Policy Constraints

statements.impact_statement: bindy uses rustls (with the ring or aws-lc-rs backend) for all TLS operations and does not link against or call into OpenSSL or libssl. The container base images (distroless/cc-debian12, chainguard/glibc-dynamic) do not include libssl. This CVE is not applicable.
bindy does not use OpenSSL for TLS or certificate handling; it uses the Rust rustls crate exclusively. No PKCS#12 parsing via OpenSSL occurs. The container base images do not include libssl.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-2961
vulnerability.description: glibc: Out-of-bounds write in iconv() with ISO-2022-CN-EXT charset

statements.impact_statement: bindy does not call the glibc iconv() function and does not process ISO-2022-CN-EXT encoded data. All string and character set handling uses Rust standard library types (str, String, Vec<u8>). This CVE is not reachable through bindy's code paths.
